text: Upon entrance to the abdominal cavity through laparoscopic ports, the surgeon clears a significant amount of pus and bile. The video shows inflammation and erythema throughout the abdominal cavity, including the abdominal wall, gallbladder, and liver, with visible fibrinous material indicating a recent perforation.

The surgeon removes the omentum covering the perforated peptic ulcer, revealing a small ulcer approximately two millimeters in width. Using an indoor stitch, the surgeon places full-thickness sutures to close the ulcer primarily. The omentum is then positioned over the closure to buttress the repair, ensuring adequate approximation of the tissue.
The surgeon completes the repair of the perforated peptic ulcer and irrigates the abdominal cavity with ten liters of normal saline, resulting in a visibly clean surgical field.
